item.title: 【Understanding MCP Architecture: LLM + API vs Model Context Protocol】了解 MCP 架构：LLM + API 与模型上下文协议
item.description:
【

Suppose you want a chatbot that works with PDFs: extract text, search across documents, summarize sections. You can build it two ways: by calling an LLM API directly and wiring tools yourself, or by exposing those tools through the Model Context Protocol (MCP). Same user experience — different architecture. This article uses a PDF example to walk through both routes and explain what MCP adds.

The Goal

User asks in natural language → chatbot reads/searches PDFs → returns an answer.

】

假设您想要一个可处理 PDF 的聊天机器人：提取文本、跨文档搜索、总结部分。您可以通过两种方式构建它：直接调用 LLM API 并自行连接工具，或者通过模型上下文协议 (MCP) 公开这些工具。相同的用户体验 - 不同的架构。本文使用 PDF 示例来介绍这两种路线并解释 MCP 添加的内容。

目标

用户用自然语言提问 → 聊天机器人读取/搜索 PDF → 返回答案。

item.title: 【How to Log HTTP Incoming Requests in Spring Boot】如何在 Spring Boot 中记录 HTTP 传入请求
item.description:
【

In developing REST APIs, you often need to log HTTP incoming requests. You want to see exactly what data your application is receiving and how it is processed. You want a detailed view of the passed data to ease troubleshooting and development. CommonsRequestLoggingFilter is a class of Spring Boot that allows you to log requests with simple configuration steps.

In this article, you'll see how to configure request logging in Spring Boot and inspect request payloads and parameters.

】

在开发 REST API 时，您通常需要记录HTTP 传入请求。您希望准确了解您的应用程序正在接收哪些数据以及如何处理这些数据。您需要详细查看所传递的数据以简化故障排除和开发。 CommonsRequestLoggingFilter 是 Spring Boot 的类，允许您通过简单的配置步骤记录请求。

在本文中，您将了解如何在 Spring Boot 中配置请求日志记录并检查请求负载和参数。

item.title: 【Unlocking Smart Meter Insights with Smart Datastream】通过智能数据流解锁智能电表洞察
item.description:
【

The rollout of smart meters across the UK has fundamentally changed how energy data is generated and used. Millions of devices now capture consumption data at fine-grained intervals, offering a much clearer picture of how energy is used across households and businesses.

This shift creates a real opportunity. With the right tools, organizations can move beyond basic reporting and start making informed decisions around efficiency, cost optimization, and sustainability.

】

英国智能电表的推出从根本上改变了能源数据的生成和使用方式。现在，数以百万计的设备以细粒度的间隔捕获消耗数据，从而更清晰地了解家庭和企业的能源使用情况。

这种转变创造了一个真正的机会。借助正确的工具，组织可以超越基本报告，开始围绕效率、成本优化和可持续性做出明智的决策。

item.title: 【Designing a Production-Grade Multi-Agent LLM Architecture for Structured Data Extraction】设计用于结构化数据提取的生产级多代理 LLM 架构
item.description:
【

Problem statement:

Many enterprise systems rely on large volumes of documents that are similar in purpose but inconsistent in structure. For example, in the field of medicare insurance, different carriers, vendors, or partners publish documents describing comparable offerings, but each uses its own format, terminology, layouts, unstructured conditional clauses, etc. 

】

问题陈述：

许多企业系统依赖于大量用途相似但结构不一致的文档。例如，在医疗保险领域，不同的运营商、供应商或合作伙伴发布了描述可比产品的文档，但每个都使用自己的格式、术语、布局、非结构化条件条款等。

item.title: 【Why Playwright Gets Blocked After 200 Requests (And What To Do About It)】为什么 Playwright 在 200 个请求后被屏蔽（以及该怎么办）
item.description:
【

The problem was not Playwright. The problem was that every layer of my connection was telling a different story about who I was.

Two Layers, One Identity

Anti-bot systems like Cloudflare, PerimeterX (now HUMAN), and Akamai do not just look at your IP address. They correlate two separate identity signals against each other.

】

问题不在于Playwright。问题是我的每一层联系都在讲述一个关于我是谁的不同故事。

两层，一个身份

Cloudflare、PerimeterX（现为 HUMAN）和 Akamai 等反机器人系统不仅仅查看您的 IP 地址。它们将两个独立的身份信号相互关联。

item.title: 【Bucket4j + Infinispan: A Deep Dive Into Implementation】Bucket4j + Infinispan：深入实施
item.description:
【

In distributed systems, the biggest challenge for rate limiting is state. How do you ensure that two parallel requests hitting different cluster nodes don't "double-spend" the same token?

In this article, we dive into the implementation details of the integration between the Bucket4j rate-limiting framework and Embedded Infinispan (not HotRod). This setup creates a data grid across different pods of a single application, allowing for seamless, distributed token management.

】

在分布式系统中，速率限制的最大挑战是状态。如何确保到达不同集群节点的两个并行请求不会“双花”同一个令牌？

在本文中，我们将深入探讨 Bucket4j 速率限制框架与嵌入式 Infinispan（而非 HotRod）之间集成的实现细节。此设置跨单个应用程序的不同 Pod 创建数据网格，从而实现无缝的分布式令牌管理。

item.title: 【6 Integration Patterns That Look Good on Paper and What Happens When They Hit Production】表面上看起来不错的 6 种集成模式以及它们投入生产后会发生什么
item.description:
【

In most enterprise systems, integrations don’t fail immediately. They fail slowly. Everything works fine at first, APIs respond quickly, workflows look clean, and dependencies seem manageable. Then traffic grows, systems evolve, and edge cases appear. That’s when the cracks start to show.

In my experience, these failures are rarely caused by tools. They come from how integration patterns are applied without considering real-world conditions like latency, retries, partial failures, and security boundaries.

】

在大多数企业系统中，集成不会立即失败。他们慢慢失败。一开始一切都运行良好，API 响应迅速，工作流程看起来干净，依赖关系似乎易于管理。然后流量增长，系统发展，边缘情况出现。这就是裂缝开始显现的时候。

根据我的经验，这些失败很少是由工具引起的。它们来自集成模式的应用方式，而不考虑延迟、重试、部分失败和安全边界等现实条件。

item.title: 【Generate Random Test Data in PostgreSQL】在 PostgreSQL 中生成随机测试数据
item.description:
【

When developing and testing applications that use a PostgreSQL database, it's often helpful to populate your tables with random data. Whether you're testing queries, performance, or database functionality, having a set of test data can help ensure your application performs as expected.

In this guide, we'll walk through how to create an anonymous PL/pgSQL block that generates random data and inserts it into a PostgreSQL table. The data will include various types such as integers, strings, dates, booleans, and UUIDs.

】

在开发和测试使用 PostgreSQL 数据库的应用程序时，使用随机数据填充表通常很有帮助。无论您是在测试查询、性能还是数据库功能，拥有一组测试数据都可以帮助确保您的应用程序按预期执行。

在本指南中，我们将介绍如何创建匿名 PL/pgSQL 块来生成随机数据并将其插入到 PostgreSQL 表中。数据将包括各种类型，例如整数、字符串、日期、布尔值和 UUID。

item.title: 【From SDLC to ADLC in AI】AI 中从 SDLC 到 ADLC
item.description:
【

BLUF: The software development lifecycle was built for a world where software shipped on a schedule. Unfortunately, our AI-augmented world doesn’t work on a schedule. CIOs who keep mapping AI initiatives onto SDLC assumptions are doing worse than just slowing things down. They’re structurally misaligned with how value actually gets created now, and the gap between organizations that figure this out and the ones that don’t is widening faster than most people realize.

The pattern is undeniable. The AI pilot lands well. Leadership gets excited. Someone stands up a project team, drops it into the delivery framework, and the whole thing slows to a crawl. Eighteen months later, the board asks what happened, and the honest answer nobody gives is: we tried to deliver a different kind of work using a process designed for a different era.

】

BLUF： 软件开发生命周期是为软件按计划交付的世界而构建的。不幸的是，我们的人工智能增强世界并不按计划运行。不断将人工智能计划映射到 SDLC 假设的首席信息官们所做的不仅仅是放慢速度。它们在结构上与现在实际创造价值的方式不一致，并且弄清楚这一点的组织与没有意识到这一点的组织之间的差距正在比大多数人意识到的更快地扩大。

这种模式是不可否认的。人工智能飞行员着陆顺利。领导很兴奋。有人成立了一个项目团队，将其放入交付框架中，整个事情就慢了下来。十八个月后，董事会询问发生了什么事，没有人给出诚实的答案：我们试图使用为不同时代设计的流程来交付不同类型的工作。

item.title: 【Clean Code: Concurrency Patterns, Context Management, and Goroutine Safety, Part 5】简洁代码：并发模式、上下文管理和 Goroutine 安全，第 5 部分
item.description:
【

Introduction: Why Go Concurrency Is Special

I've debugged goroutine leaks at 3 AM, fixed race conditions that only appeared under load, and watched a single missing defer statement bring down a production service. "Don't communicate by sharing memory; share memory by communicating" — this Go mantra turned concurrent programming on its head. Instead of mutexes and semaphores, channels. Instead of threads — goroutines. Instead of callbacks — select. And all this with context for lifecycle management.

Common concurrency mistakes I've encountered:

】

简介：为什么 Go 并发很特别

我在凌晨 3 点调试了 Goroutine 泄漏，修复了仅在负载下出现的竞争条件，并观察到单个缺失的 defer 语句导致生产服务瘫痪。 “不要通过共享内存来通信；通过通信来共享内存”——这句 Go 格言彻底颠覆了并发编程。通道取代了互斥体和信号量。而不是线程 - goroutine。而不是回调——选择。所有这些都与生命周期管理相关。

我遇到的常见并发错误：
